description: INS-728 (no-false-positive branch): fal.ai has no dedicated n8n credential, so the resolver returns 'none' and no PREFER_PREDEFINED_CREDENTIAL warning should fire. The builder must leave the HTTP Request node on a generic credential and must NOT force it onto a predefined credential type or invent a fal.ai credential. Guards against the steering over-firing on unknown / self-hosted hosts. Build-only. Should PASS both with and without the PR — its job is to prove the PR does not regress the unknown-host case.

conversation.text: Using an HTTP Request node, call the fal.ai API at https://queue.fal.run/fal-ai/flux to generate an image from a text prompt. Authenticate with an API key sent in the Authorization header as 'Key <my-api-key>'. Let me trigger it manually.

outcomeExpectations: The HTTP Request node calling fal.ai (queue.fal.run) authenticates with a generic credential — authentication 'genericCredentialType' (e.g. httpHeaderAuth or httpCustomAuth carrying the Authorization header).

The node must NOT use authentication 'predefinedCredentialType', and must NOT reference any fal.ai-specific credential type (n8n has none). No dedicated-credential steering should be applied to this unknown host.
